SEARCH
Package dev.jeka.core.api.file

Class JkAbstractPathTree<T extends JkAbstractPathTree>

java.lang.Object
dev.jeka.core.api.file.JkAbstractPathTree<T>
Type Parameters:
T - for self returning keeping the actual type.
Direct Known Subclasses:
JkPathTree, JkZipTree
public class JkAbstractPathTree<T extends JkAbstractPathTree> extends Object
Provides a view on files and sub-folders contained in a given directory or zip file. A JkPathTree may have some include/exclude filters to include only or exclude specified files.
When speaking about files contained in a JkAbstractPathTree, we mean all files contained in its root directory or subdirectories, matching positively the filter defined on it.

    A decorator class for Stream that provides additional functionality specific to path trees.

    Creates a JkAbstractPathTree having the specified root directory.

    Assuming the root folder is relative, this creates an identical JkAbstractPathTree but having the root as : [specified new root]/[former root]

    • of

      public static JkAbstractPathTree of(Path rootDir)

    • newInstance

    • withRoot

      protected T withRoot(Path newRoot)

    • getRoot

      public Path getRoot()
      Returns the root directory. In case of zip archive it returns a directory entry within the zip archive.

    • getMatcher

      public JkPathMatcher getMatcher()
      Returns the filter defined on this JkAbstractPathTree, never null.

    • hasFilter

      public boolean hasFilter()

    • exists

      public boolean exists()
      Returns true if the root directory exists.

    • createIfNotExist

      public T createIfNotExist()
      Creates root directory if not exists.

    • stream

      public JkAbstractPathTree<T>.JkPathTreeStream stream(FileVisitOption... options)
      Returns a stream of paths in the directory tree rooted at the specified directory, filtered by the specified options. The result contains folders, including root.

      The tree is traversed using a deep first algorithm.

      The returned paths are NOT relatives to the root of this tree.

      The returned stream provides means o filer file only, and transform to paths relative to root dir.

    • streamBreathFirst

      public JkAbstractPathTree<T>.JkPathTreeStream streamBreathFirst()
      Same as stream(FileVisitOption...) but using a breath-first traversal algorithm.

    • getRelativeFiles

      @Deprecated public List<Path> getRelativeFiles()
      Deprecated.
      Use stream(FileVisitOption...) in combination with JkAbstractPathTree.JkPathTreeStream.relativizeFromRoot() instead
      Same as getFiles() but returning paths relative to this tree root.

    • getFiles

      public List<Path> getFiles()
      Returns list of paths returned by stream(FileVisitOption...) but excluding directories from the result.

    • goTo

      public T goTo(String relativePath)
      Creates a JkAbstractPathTree having the specified relative path to this root as getRoot directory. Note that the returned tree has no filter even if this tree has one.

    • resolvedTo

      public T resolvedTo(Path newRoot)
      Assuming the root folder is relative, this creates an identical JkAbstractPathTree but having the root as : [specified new root]/[former root]

    • get

      public Path get(String relativePath)
      Returns path relative to this root of the specified relative path.

    • importDir

      public T importDir(Path dirToCopy, CopyOption... copyOptions)
      Copies the specified directory and its content at the root of this tree.

    • importTree

      public T importTree(JkAbstractPathTree tree, CopyOption... copyOptions)
      Copies the content of the specified tree at the root of this one. Specified dir to copy to might not exist. The structure of the specified tree is preserved. Note that the root of the specified tree is not part of the copied content.

    • importFiles

      public T importFiles(Iterable<Path> files, StandardCopyOption... copyOptions)
      Copies the specified files at the root of this tree. The copy is not recursive.

    • importFile

      public T importFile(Path src, String targetName, StandardCopyOption... copyOptions)
      Copies the specified file at the specified path within this tree.

    • deleteContent

      public T deleteContent()
      Deletes each and every file in this tree except the root and files not matching this tree filter.

    • deleteRoot

      public T deleteRoot()
      Deletes the root directory of the path tree. This method also deletes all the content inside the root directory.

    • deleteRootIfExist

      public T deleteRootIfExist()
      Deletes the root directory of the path tree. This method also deletes all the content inside the root directory.

    • zipTo

      public T zipTo(Path destination)
      Zips the content of this tree to the specified destination file. If the specified destination file already exists, the content of this tree is appended to the existing archive, overriding existing entries within the archive.

    • copyTo

      public int copyTo(Path destinationDir, CopyOption... copyOptions)
      Copies files contained in this JkAbstractPathTree to the specified directory.

    • copyFile

      public void copyFile(String sourcePath, Path destinationDir, CopyOption... copyOptions)
      Copies a single file contained in this JkAbstractPathTree to the specified directory. File name remains the same.
      Parameters:
      sourcePath - The relative path of the source file from this tree root.

    • andMatcher

      public T andMatcher(PathMatcher pathMatcher)
      Creates a copy of this JkAbstractPathTree augmented with the specified JkPathMatcher

    • withMatcher

      public T withMatcher(JkPathMatcher pathMatcher)
      Creates a JkAbstractPathTree which is a copy of this JkAbstractPathTree but the matcher is replaced with the specified one.

    • andMatching

      public T andMatching(boolean positive, String... globPatterns)
      Creates a copy of this JkAbstractPathTree augmented with the specified pattern matcher.

    • andMatching

      public T andMatching(String... globPatterns)
      Shorthand to andMatching(true, globPatterns...).

    • andMatching

      public T andMatching(boolean positive, Iterable<String> globPatterns)
      Creates a copy of this JkAbstractPathTree augmented with the specified pattern matcher.

    • count

      public int count(int max, boolean includeDirectories)
      Returns the file count contained in this JkAbstractPathTree to concurrence to specified max count. If the effective count is greater than max count, this method returns max count + 1. This method is designed to stop file traversal as soon as count is greater than max.

    • containFiles

      public boolean containFiles()

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • watch

      public void watch(long millis, AtomicBoolean run, Consumer<List<JkAbstractPathTree.FileChange>> fileChangeConsumer)
      Watches file and directory changes involved in this path tree. This method blocks for the specified millis time to capture filesystem changes and pass them to the specified consumer.
      Parameters:
      millis - Time to wait for capturing file changes
      run - Semaphore, this methods loop until this values false
      fileChangeConsumer - The consumer to be invoked on file changes

    • watch

      public void watch(long millis, AtomicBoolean run, Runnable runnable)
      Same as watch(long, AtomicBoolean, Consumer) but just triggering a consumer on file changes.

    • watch

      public void watch(long millis, Runnable runnable)
      Same as watch(long, AtomicBoolean, Runnable) but running indefinitely.

    • checksum

      public String checksum(String algorithm)
      Computes the checksum of this tree content. This includes file contents and file structure. This means that changing a file name without but keeping its content unchanged will produce distinct checksum.
      Parameters:
      algorithm - algorithm used to produce checksum, such as md5 or sha256.
